thread.title: ‘It Would Be Like A Tasteless Dish’ – Former Pakistan Cricketer’s Unnecessary Claim On Champions Trophy 2025
thread.title_full: ‘It Would Be Like A Tasteless Dish’ – Former Pakistan Cricketer’s Unnecessary Claim On Champions Trophy 2025

author: Saksham Nagar
title: ‘It Would Be Like A Tasteless Dish’ – Former Pakistan Cricketer’s Unnecessary Claim On Champions Trophy 2025
text:
As the row on the ICC Champions Trophy 2025 is still, BCCI is still pretty much rigid on not playing the tournament scheduled to be held in Pakistan, whereas PCB has also not taken a backfoot and are firm on hosting the competition in their country itself. Several reports arise on daily basis wherein on one end there are claims that ICC is trying to convince the Pakistan Cricket Board to host the tournament under the hybrid model. On the other hand, there were also reports that the officers from both the cricket boards are expected to hold a meeting and discuss issues related to the ICC Champions Trophy 2025.
Also Read: IND vs AUS 1st Test: Jasprit Bumrah Led Pace Attack Script Australia’s Batting Downfall After Being Wrapped Up For 150 Runs
Basit Ali’s Big Claim On ICC Champions Trophy 2025 Former Pakistan cricketer Basit Ali while speaking on his YouTube channel stated that the broadcasters won’t agree if the Champions Trophy is held under the hybrid model. Basit added that if the Champions Trophy is held under hybrid model, India and Pakistan won’t be in the same group which will make the tournament a ‘tasteless dish’. He said, “What did I tell you about the Champions Trophy… Broadcaster isn’t agreeing to (hybrid model or India and Pakistan in different pools and it won’t. If there is no India-Pakistan match in a tournament, then it would be like a tasteless dish.”
Also Read: IND Vs AUS: Virat Kohli Tops The List Of Catches Dropped By An Indian Since 2019! Check Full Report
“PCB has played its cards very well, well done! If you want to go ahead with the Hybrid Model, do it. But India and Pakistan won’t be in the same pool, and Pakistan will play all their matches at home. Mohsin Naqvi is quietly sitting now. He has played his cards correctly” , Basit Ali added.
However, the International Cricket Council is yet to make a decision on the ICC Champions Trophy and was reported to release the schedule of the tournament by the end of this week. However, as the circumstances are set, it is very unlikely for ICC to release the schedule at the current time.
